title: Microfluidic aptasensor including a graphene nanosensor
abstract: Graphene nanosensors for monitoring a target analyte utilizing anti-target analyte aptamers can include a single conductance sensor on a substrate platform, where the graphene sensor can be functionalized with aptamers for binding the target analyte, or alternatively, a nonosensor can include microbeads functionalized with aptamers which can allow for selective enrichment and isocratic elution of the target analyte, where the concentration of the enriched target analyte can be measured on a graphene surface functionalized with a target analyte of interest.
claims: Claims 1. A microdevice for monitoring a target analyte using at least one aptamer that binds to a target analyte, the microdevice comprising: a substrate platform and a nanosensor, wherein the nanosensor is coupled to the substrate platform and comprises a conductance element functionalized with an aptamer for detecting the target analyte. 2. The microdevice of claim 1, wherein the conductive element comprises an element having a surface adapted for a change in charge density thereon upon the binding of the aptamer with the target analyte. 3. The microdevice of claim 1, wherein the conductive element comprises graphene. 4. The microdevice of claim 3, wherein the graphene comprises a single layer sheet. 5. The microdevice of claim 1, wherein the nanosensor further comprises a gate electrode formed from a nanolayer including at least one of ITO, Ti/Pd/Pt, gold, chrome or copper. 6. The microdevice of claim 3, wherein the nanosensor further comprises a source electrode and a drain electrode, and wherein the graphene sheet makes contact to both the source electrode and the drain electrode. 7. The microdevice of claim 6, wherein the source and drain electrodes comprise a nanolayer including at least one of ITO, Ti/Pd/Pt, chromium, gold, or chrome. 8. The microdevice of claim 1, wherein the platform substrate comprises at least one of polyethylene terephthalate (PET), polycarbonate polystyrene, polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA), polymacon, silicones, fluoropolymers, silicone acrylate, fluoro-silicone/acrylate, or poly hydroxyethyl methacrylate. 9. The microdevice of claim 1, wherein the target analyte comprises a small organic, peptide, protein, cell, or tissue. 10. The microdevice of claim 1, wherein the aptamer is a single-stranded oligonucleotide or a peptide. 11. The microdevice of claim 10, wherein the aptamer is a single-stranded oligonucleotide comprising DNA, RNA, XNA, or a combination thereof. 12. The microdevice of claim 3, wherein the aptamer is functionalized to the surface of graphene via a linker. 13. The microdevice of claim 12, wherein the linker is a pyrene-terminated agent. 14. The microdevice of claim 13, wherein the the pyrene-terminated agent is 1- pyrenebutanoic acid succinimidyl ester. 15. The microdevice of claim 12, wherein the graphene is serially functionalized with a polymer and aptamers. 16. The microdevice of claim 15, wherein one end of the polymer is coupled to the aptamer and the other end of the polymer is coupled to the linker. 17. The microdevice of claim 15, wherein the polymer is polyethlene glycol. 18. A microdevice for monitoring a target analyte comprising: a support platform, at least one enrichment chamber, wherein the enrichment chamber comprises microbeads functionalized with an aptamer that binds target analyte; at least one sensing chamber, wherein the sensing chamber comprises a graphene nanosensor functionalized with control target analyte; and at least one serpentine channel connecting the at least one enrichment chamber and the at least one sensing chamber. 19. The microdevice of claim 18, wherein the graphene comprises a single layer sheet. 20. The microdevice of claim 18, wherein the graphene nanosensor further comprises a gate electrode formed from a nanolayer including at least one of ITO, Ti/Pd/Pt, gold, chrome or copper. 21. The microdevice of claim 20, wherein the graphene nanosensor further comprises a source electrode and a drain electrode, and wherein the graphene sheet makes contact to both the source electrode and the drain electrode. 22. The microdevice of claim 21, wherein the source and drain electrodes comprise a nanolayer including at least one of ITO, Ti/Pd/Pt, chromium, gold, or chrome. 23. The microdevice of claim 18, wherein the support platform substrate comprises at least one of polyethylene terephthalate (PET), polycarbonate polystyrene, polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA), polymacon, silicones, fluoropolymers, silicone acrylate, fluoro-silicone/acrylate, or poly hydroxy ethyl methacrylate. 24. The microdevice of claim 18, wherein the target analyte comprises a small organic, peptide, protein, cell, or tissue. 25. The microdevice of claim 18, wherein the aptamer is a single-stranded oligonucleotide or a peptide. 26. The microdevice of claim 25, wherein the aptamer is a single-stranded oligonucleotide comprising DNA, RNA, XNA, or a combination thereof. 27. The microdevice of claim 18, wherein the aptamer is functionalized to the surface of microbead via a linker. 28. The microdevice of claim 27, wherein the linker is a pyrene-terminated agent. 29. The microdevice of claim 28, wherein the the pyrene-terminated agent is 1 - pyrenebutanoic acid succinimidyl ester. 30. The microdevice of claim 18, further comprising a temperature control unit for each enrichment chamber, each sensing chamber, and each serpentine channel. 31. A method for monitoring a target analyte using an aptamer capable of binding to the target analyte, comprising: placing a nanosensor in contact with a sample containing a target analyte, wherein the nanosensor comprises a conductance element functionalized with an aptamer for detecting the target analyte; detecting a change, if any, in the conductance of the conductance element; and electrodebased on the detected change, determining a presence and/or concentration of the target analyte in the sample. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the binding of the aptamer with the target analyte causes a change in the charge density on the conductive element surface. 33. The method of claim 31, wherein the aptamer reversibly binds with the target analyte. 34. The method of claim 31, wherein the nanodevice detects low concentrations of the target analyte. 35. A method for monitoring a target analyte using an aptamer capable of binding to the target analyte, comprising: placing a device in contact with a sample containing a target analyte, wherein the device comprises at least one enrichment chamber; at least one sensing chamber comprising a conductance element; and at least one serpentine channel connecting the at least one enrichment chamber and the at least one sensing chamber; enriching the target analyte in the enriching chamber; releasing the enriched target analyte from the enriching chamber; moving the enriched target analyte to the sensing chamber; detecting a change, if any, in the conductance of the conductance element in the sensing chamber; and based on the detected change, determining a presence and/or concentration of the target analyte in the sample. 36. The method of claim 35, further comprising removing the nonspecifically adsorbed target analyte and impurities from the enriching chamber prior to releasing the enriched target analyte. 37. The method of claim 35, further comprising adding a solution of free aptamer to the enriching chamber following release of the target analyte. 38. The method of claim 35, wherein the binding of the aptamer with the control target analyte causes a change in the charge density on the conductive element surface.
